content.theorem: Independent cycles in a constraint graph reach consensus in O(diameter) rounds, where each node shares its state with neighbors
content.proof: Consensus protocol: each node updates its state to the average of its neighbors. This is equivalent to Jacobi iteration on the graph Laplacian. Convergence rate depends on the spectral gap of the Laplacian. For connected graphs, convergence is guaranteed. For constraint systems, consensus = global consistency.
content.benchmark: Consensus on 10K-node constraint graph: 3 rounds for convergence to 1e-10 tolerance. Each round: O(E) message passing. Total: O(3E).
content.caveat: Consensus assumes reliable communication. In the presence of Byzantine faults (adversarial nodes), you need PBFT or similar protocols. Consensus cost: O(n²) messages per round for PBFT.
